pages.full_text:
Next time we cross to France we think we might camp in Kent & drive on to camp near Montreuil & wander up to the Town in the afternoon & have supper.

The loo block needs renewing. A swimming pool. The big field we camped in had one other family where the woman talked in a loud voice all the time. The woman running the site organised bingo in the evening. Her family owned it (maybe from the château, she was a law student & spent last year in Turkey with Erasmus. Why do northen French families damage campsites near Calais?

__Saturday August 11th__
We passed by St. Gabriel & Cécile & walked along a huge sandy beach - Côte d'Opale,
place_mentions: Palais de l'Abbé Jean de Bourbon
pages.notes: Page includes a glued-in colour postcard of Abbaye de Cluny and a printed ticket (BILLET JUMELE) dated 09/08/2012 for Abbaye de Cluny and Palais de l'Abbé Jean de Bourbon. The ticket date 09/08/2012 confirms the year as 2012. 'northen' is the diary's spelling. Text continues from a previous page. 'St. Gabriel' could refer to St-Gabriel-Brécy or a beach/locality on the Côte d'Opale — context suggests the latter.

pages.full_text:
opat-coloured, like St. Tropez, which we passed earlier. Hideously sandy. Oh! for Suffolk shingle! Then on past Boulogne to the Eurotunnel. Our train was supposed to be 12:06. but it left at 12:13. We are wondering whether the end-of-Olympic traffic will enable us to get to the kennels before 4 pm to get Tinder. -In fact, the kennels have changed their time to 4-5, so we managed to fetd him. On the way we stopped for a cup of tea at Aston Rowant, a nature reserve for watching red kites in Oxfordshire off the M40.

A super trip.

__August 30th Friday.__     __Bourton Westwood Farm__
8.30. Simon, Eve & Tinder set off for a farm campsite **** South west of Much Wenlock. The site was set up years ago by the aunt of the present owner's [wife?]. The field was where the house [cow?] lived. They walked up the lane each day to milk her. Finally [they] dug a pit for a loo & put in a tip the other side of
pages.notes: Top-right corner shows what appears to be a partial number — possibly clipped by the scan boundary. 'opat-coloured' likely means 'opal-coloured' (diary spelling). 'fetd' is the diary's spelling of 'fetch' (or 'fetched'). The reference to 'end-of-Olympic traffic' confirms 2012 (London Olympics ended 12 Aug 2012). 'August 30th Friday' as 2012-08-30: 30 August 2012 was a Thursday — the diarist may have the day wrong, or this could be 2013 (30 Aug 2013 was a Friday); however the surrounding context strongly suggests 2012, so noting the discrepancy here. 'Bourton Westwood Farm' is underlined and appears as a heading. A word after 'present owner's' is unclear — possibly 'wife'. Text cuts off at the bottom of the page.
